vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1845: remote Fly work-agent durability & resiliency tiers

plan.title: CRITICAL: remote Fly work agents lose ALL work on crash — durability + resiliency tiers
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: A remote Fly work agent (PAN-1762) died abnormally on Fly's host and lost 100% of its generated code and compute spend: durability was push-once-at-completion, and the machine had no volume + ephemeral rootfs (resets on every start) + restart.policy:'no'. Any abnormal death before completion vaporizes the unpushed branch and the disk, with no recovery path. This blocks the entire remote-execution feature.
narratives.Proposal: Land the operator-agreed durability fixes before remote Fly work agents run in production again: (#1) a VM-side continuous commit+push heartbeat daemon making origin the durable store (both tiers); (#2) a persistent Fly volume mounted at /workspace (durable tier); (3a) restart.policy:'on-failure' + bounded max_retries (durable tier); a user-selectable resiliency tier (ephemeral vs durable) via config + CLI flag + dashboard; a spend guardrail (max concurrent remote agents); and a durability preflight gate that refuses remote work-agent spawn unless the tier's durability mechanisms are wired.
narratives.NonGoals:
- 3c supervisor-as-PID-1 (the self-healing intended end-state) — separate follow-up issue.
- #4 machine-health monitoring / abnormal-death alerting / 'unpushed for N min' health warning — explicitly future per the operator comment.
- Host-independent auto-RELAUNCH of the agent after a restart, and restoring the Claude conversation JSONL post-restart (work resumes from filesystem + origin with a fresh session).
- Migrating CLIProxy / non-Anthropic (gpt-5.5, codex, kimi) agents to remote — remote stays claude-code + Anthropic-only.
- Sharing one volume across multiple machines concurrently.
- A host-side push patrol (redundant with the VM-side daemon and the existing salvageRemoteWork crash path).

autoDecisions.summary: Scope = the operator-agreed required set (#1 continuous push, #2 volume, 3a restart, resiliency-tier setting, spend cap, durability gate); 3c supervisor and #4 monitoring deferred to follow-ups.
autoDecisions.rationale: The operator comment named #1+#2 'required' and set the production gate at '#1+#2 land'; 3c is explicitly 'more work / intended end-state' and #4 'future / out of immediate scope'.

autoDecisions.summary: Continuous push = VM-side detached tmux heartbeat daemon (commit+push every N min) for both tiers, plus a per-bead push instruction in work.md. No host-side push patrol.
autoDecisions.rationale: Only a VM-side daemon survives the durable tier's laptop-closed promise AND is independent of agent diligence (the crash that loses work is exactly when the agent never pushed). Host patrol is redundant with the daemon + existing salvage.
Cheapest, safest cost posture; matches the operator's bankruptcy-risk concern that a 100-agent fleet must not silently run forever.

autoDecisions.summary: Ephemeral wind-down = host-heartbeat + VM-side watchdog self-stop (kills PID 1); durable tier installs no watchdog.
autoDecisions.rationale: An autonomous work agent does not go idle when the laptop closes, so idle-stop is insufficient; a heartbeat watchdog is the bounded mechanism that does not require the full PID-1 supervisor.

autoDecisions.summary: 3a delivers durability (work survives via volume + continuous push), not host-independent agent auto-relaunch; resume verified by the volume-survives-stop->start test.
autoDecisions.rationale: Re-launching the agent after restart and restoring the Claude JSONL (on the wiped rootfs) require the 3c supervisor; the acceptance gate is '#1+#2 land', which durability satisfies.

autoDecisions.summary: Issue-body item #4 (operator visibility) vs operator comment (#4 = future) resolved in favor of the operator comment.
autoDecisions.rationale: The operator comment is later, authoritative, and explicit; not a blocking contradiction, so --auto proceeds. Basic visibility (tier surfaced in settings) is still delivered.

items.title: Add Fly volume CRUD to FlyApiClient

narrative.Action: Add createVolume/getVolume/listVolumes/deleteVolume methods to FlyApiClient in src/lib/remote/fly-api.ts, wrapping the /apps/{app}/volumes Machines API endpoints, plus Effect-typed wrappers matching the existing createMachine/destroyMachine pattern.

items.title: createVolume(app, {name, region, sizeGb}) sends POST /apps/{app}/volumes and returns the created volume id
deleteVolume(app, volumeId) sends DELETE /apps/{app}/volumes/{id}; getVolume and listVolumes send the matching GET requests
Each Effect-typed wrapper fails with FlyApiError on an HTTP error, matching the existing createMachine wrapper
A unit test with mocked fetch asserts each method sends the correct method, path, and body
Add mounts and restart.max_retries to FlyMachineConfig and createMachine

narrative.Action: Extend FlyMachineConfig in src/lib/remote/fly-api.ts with an optional mounts array and a restart.max_retries field, and update createMachine to include both in the POST config body when present, leaving today's payload unchanged when they are omitted.

items.title: FlyMachineConfig accepts mounts as an array of {volume, path} and restart.max_retries as a number
createMachine includes mounts and restart.max_retries in the request body when they are provided
Omitting mounts and max_retries produces the current payload unchanged (no mounts key, restart policy:no)
A unit test asserts the createMachine body contains mounts and max_retries when passed
Add resiliency_tier and max_concurrent_agents to remote config

narrative.Action: Add resiliency_tier ('ephemeral'|'durable') and max_concurrent_agents to RemoteConfig/RemoteFlyConfig in src/lib/config.ts, with config normalization defaulting resiliency_tier to 'ephemeral' and validating the enum, so loadConfig round-trips both fields from yaml.

items.title: RemoteConfig accepts resiliency_tier ('ephemeral'|'durable') and max_concurrent_agents (number)
Config normalization defaults to ephemeral when resiliency_tier is unset and rejects an out-of-enum value
loadConfig round-trips both fields from yaml and preserves them on save
A unit test asserts the default tier defaults to ephemeral and an invalid tier value is rejected
Thread resiliency tier into FlyProvider.createVm

narrative.Action: Add resiliencyTier to FlyProviderConfig in src/lib/remote/fly-provider.ts (constructor default 'ephemeral'), map RemoteConfig.resiliency_tier into it in createFlyProviderFromConfig (src/lib/remote/index.ts), and read this.config.resiliencyTier inside createVm so the volume and restart branches can key off it without changing today's ephemeral payload.

items.title: FlyProviderConfig accepts resiliencyTier and the constructor defaults it to ephemeral
createFlyProviderFromConfig routes RemoteConfig.resiliency_tier (and an explicit override) into FlyProviderConfig.resiliencyTier
createVm reads this.config.resiliencyTier and the ephemeral-tier create payload preserves today's restart policy:no with no mounts
A unit test asserts the ephemeral createVm payload preserves restart:no and contains no mounts entry
Mount a persistent volume at /workspace for the durable tier

narrative.Action: In FlyProvider.createVm, for the durable tier ensure a region-pinned volume exists (create by deterministic per-issue name if absent) and add a mounts:[{volume, path:'/workspace'}] entry to the create payload; for the ephemeral tier create no volume and add no mounts; on the get-or-create adopt path refuse to run durable work on a machine missing the expected volume.

items.title: Durable-tier createVm creates a region-pinned volume (when absent) and passes a mounts entry binding it to /workspace
Ephemeral-tier createVm creates no volume and the payload contains no mounts entry
On the adopt path, a durable spawn refuses (or fresh-creates) when the existing machine has no /workspace volume mounted
A unit test with a mocked Fly API asserts durable createVm creates a volume and passes mounts while ephemeral does not
Destroy the Fly volume when the machine is destroyed

narrative.Action: Update FlyProvider.deleteVm (src/lib/remote/fly-provider.ts) and the reap patrol machine-destroy path (src/lib/remote/remote-completion.ts) to delete the machine's associated per-issue volume after destroying the machine, so no orphaned volume survives handoff or teardown; a machine with no volume is a no-op.

items.title: deleteVm deletes the machine's associated volume after destroying the machine for a durable machine
Destroying an ephemeral (volumeless) machine performs no volume deletion and throws no error
The reap patrol's machine-destroy path triggers the same teardown so no orphaned volume survives after handoff
A unit test asserts deleteVm deletes the volume for a durable machine and skips it for an ephemeral one
Set restart:on-failure + bounded max_retries for the durable tier

narrative.Action: In FlyProvider.createVm, set the create payload restart block to {policy:'on-failure', max_retries:<finite constant>} for the durable tier and keep {policy:'no'} for the ephemeral tier, documenting the retry cap and never using policy:'always'.

items.title: Durable-tier createVm payload applies restart on-failure with a finite max_retries constant
Ephemeral-tier createVm payload preserves restart policy:no
The retry cap stays a bounded constant and the code never applies restart policy:always
A unit test covers the restart block per tier (on-failure+max_retries vs policy:no)
Install a VM-side continuous commit+push heartbeat daemon at spawn

narrative.Action: In spawnRemoteAgent (src/lib/remote/remote-agents.ts), install a detached tmux heartbeat session on the VM (separate from the agent session) running a loop that every N minutes commits any /workspace changes with a wip(remote): prefix and pushes the feature branch to origin; install it for both tiers, log push failures to a VM file without killing the loop, and write the daemon script via the chunked writeRemoteFile path.

items.title: spawnRemoteAgent spawns a detached tmux heartbeat session distinct from the agent session for both tiers
The daemon loop commits /workspace changes with a wip(remote): prefix and pushes the current feature branch each interval
A push failure logs to a VM-side file and the loop survives (the next interval still pushes)
A fake-timers unit test of the daemon-script generator asserts it pushes the feature branch with the wip(remote): prefix at the configured interval
In a mocked-exec test the daemon pushes the feature branch to origin within the first interval
Wind down ephemeral-tier machines on stale host heartbeat

narrative.Action: Have the deacon patrol refresh a host-heartbeat freshness file on each active ephemeral-tier VM each cycle, and install a VM-side watchdog (ephemeral tier only) that self-stops the machine by killing PID 1 when the heartbeat is older than a configured threshold; durable-tier VMs get no watchdog.

items.title: The deacon patrol updates a host-heartbeat file on each active ephemeral-tier VM each cycle
The VM-side watchdog stops the machine (kills PID 1) when the heartbeat is older than the threshold
Durable-tier VMs install no watchdog and the machine survives a stale heartbeat
A fake-timers unit test covers the watchdog stopping on a stale heartbeat and not stopping on a fresh one
Instruct per-bead commit+push in the work prompt REMOTE block

narrative.Action: Update the {{#REMOTE}} block in src/lib/cloister/prompts/work.md to instruct the agent to commit and push the feature branch after completing each bead (not only at completion), while preserving the existing push-then-REMOTE_DONE-sentinel completion contract unchanged.

items.title: The rendered REMOTE block contains an instruction to commit and push the feature branch after each bead
The rendered REMOTE block preserves the existing completion contract (final push + REMOTE_DONE sentinel)
A prompt-render test shows the per-bead push instruction in the REMOTE render and absent from the LOCAL render
Enforce max concurrent remote agents at spawn

narrative.Action: Before spawning a remote work agent (the start.ts --remote path), compare the count from listActiveRemoteAgentStates against remote.max_concurrent_agents and refuse the spawn with a clear error naming the cap and current count when exceeded; an unset or zero cap means unlimited, preserving today's behavior.

items.title: The preflight refuses a remote work-agent spawn beyond max_concurrent_agents with an error naming the cap and current count
A spawn below the cap passes the preflight and proceeds normally
An unset or zero cap is treated as unlimited and preserves today's spawn behavior
A unit test covers refusal at the cap and a passing spawn below it
Refuse remote work-agent spawn unless tier durability is satisfied

narrative.Action: Add a preflight to the remote spawn path that refuses to start a work agent unless the continuous-push daemon is wired (both tiers) and, for the durable tier, a volume is mounted at /workspace; the refusal error must name the missing mechanism and the tier, and a correctly configured spawn proceeds.

items.title: The preflight refuses a durable-tier spawn with no /workspace volume mounted, with an actionable error naming the tier and missing volume
The preflight refuses a spawn with no continuous-push daemon wired for both tiers
A correctly configured spawn (push wired; durable implies volume present) passes the preflight and proceeds
A unit test asserts refusal when the durable tier lacks a volume and success when it is present
Add pan start --remote --tier flag and thread it through

narrative.Action: Add a --tier <ephemeral|durable> option to pan start (src/cli/commands/start.ts), thread it through CreateRemoteWorkspaceOptions (src/lib/remote-workspace.ts) into createFlyProviderFromConfig so it overrides config.remote.resiliency_tier for that spawn, reject an invalid value, and update skills/pan-start/SKILL.md in the same change so lint-skills passes.

items.title: pan start --remote --tier durable|ephemeral accepts the value and rejects an invalid one naming the allowed set
The flag wins over config.remote.resiliency_tier for that spawn and routes through into the provider tier
Omitting --tier falls back to the config default tier
skills/pan-start/SKILL.md documents --tier and npm run lint (lint-skills) passes
Surface resiliency tier + spend cap in settings

narrative.Action: Map remote.resiliency_tier and remote.max_concurrent_agents through src/lib/settings-api.ts (GET returns, PUT persists to yaml) and add a dashboard Settings control to pick the tier and set the cap, following the pan-style-guide (no pill badges, no decorative color), so the chosen tier is visible to the operator.

items.title: Settings-api GET returns remote.resiliency_tier and remote.max_concurrent_agents
Settings-api PUT persists both fields to yaml and a subsequent GET returns the saved values
A dashboard Settings control renders the tier picker and cap input and shows the current values
A test covers the settings round-trip of both fields through GET and PUT
Document the resiliency tiers and durability model

narrative.Action: Add a resiliency-tiers section to the remote-workspace docs (docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md) covering both tiers, continuous push, the volume, restart policy, the spend cap, and the durability gate; and update .pan/context/codebase/concerns.md so the ephemeral-rootfs landmine note records the per-tier mitigation and points at this issue.

items.title: docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md contains a resiliency-tiers section describing the ephemeral and durable postures and the continuous-push guarantee
.pan/context/codebase/concerns.md contains a note that the ephemeral-rootfs landmine is mitigated per tier and references PAN-1845
The docs section contains the '#1+#2 land' production gate and the spend-cap guardrail
